title: pseuderucaria clavata subsp. clavata
intro: Pseuderucaria clavata subsp. clavata, also known as the hooked crowberry, is a small, shrub-like plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is a type of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pseuderucaria clavata subsp. clavata include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea infusions, tinctures, or as a dried extract to harness its medicinal properties.
